title: Investment Advisor
description: A well\-established international investment management firm is continuing to grow its private client business in Ireland and is seeking to hire an Investment Advisor to join its Dublin\-based team. This is a client\-facing role focused on managing and developing long\-term relationships with high\-net\-worth individuals, providing ongoing guidance, support and education around investment portfolios and market developments. **The Opportunity** You will manage a portfolio of high\-net\-worth clients, acting as their primary point of contact and trusted advisor. Working closely with internal investment and research teams, you will be responsible for: * Building and maintaining strong, long\-term client relationships * Communicating portfolio performance, investment strategy and market updates * Explaining asset allocation decisions and economic developments in a clear and accessible way * Supporting clients through different market cycles and maintaining confidence in long\-term strategies * Acting as a conduit between clients and internal teams, ensuring a high standard of service delivery Investment decisions are made centrally by a dedicated investment team, allowing you to focus on client engagement and relationship management. **What We’re Looking For** We are keen to speak with individuals who have experience working with private clients in a financial services environment. **Typical backgrounds may include:** * Wealth management / private client advisory * Financial planning * Stockbroking * Private banking **You will ideally have:** * Experience dealing directly with high\-net\-worth individuals * A strong understanding of financial markets and investment principles * The ability to communicate complex investment concepts clearly * A relationship\-focused approach with a high level of professionalism * A genuine interest in markets and client outcomes **QFA qualification (or working towards it) is required.** **Working Environment** * Flexible working model * Strong emphasis on work\-life balance and autonomy * Collaborative team environment with ongoing training and development * Clear progression opportunities based on individual strengths and interests **Compensation** * Competitive base salary * Performance\-related bonus linked to client engagement and retention * Comprehensive benefits package **Why Consider This Role?** This is an excellent opportunity for someone looking to build a long\-term career in investment advisory within a stable, client\-focused environment. The role offers the chance to work closely with high\-net\-worth clients, deepen your investment expertise, and develop as a trusted advisor without the pressure of a traditional sales\-driven role. If you’d like to explore this opportunity in confidence, please get in touch directly.

title: Investment Corporate Actions Representative III/Associate Analyst
description: **Job Description** **Corporate Actions Analyst – Income \& Class Actions.** **Location: Dublin** Perform daily tasks associated with the processing and reconciliation of voluntary and mandatory corporate actions, as well as research Class Action cases. Process corporate actions promptly and correctly with policies, procedures, and regulations. Interact with various groups across the organization as well as external vendors and business partners. The **Expertise** We’re Looking For * 1\-2 years of relevant Financial Services experience. * Mandatory and Voluntary Corporate Actions processing experience preferable. * Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent work experience. The **Purpose** of Your Role * Conduct and keep track of corporate action activities. * Conduct processing of all types of corporate action events, both voluntary and mandatory. * Facilitate the confirmation and settlement of security and currency transactions and provide daily cash estimates. * Coordinate corporate action executions, ensure the collection of income to the funds, resolve position differences, and facilitate securities lending activities. * Assist the team with corporate action research and process tasks related to assets under management. * Collaborate with stakeholders on issues and continuously follow up to resolve them. * Escalate high\-risk items or seek assistance to mitigate loss. * Develop the knowledge required to grasp product and processing rules while working under the direction of more experienced colleagues. * Display a strong sense of teamwork. * Explore and communicate new ways of improving existing processes. The **Skills** You Bring * Your knowledge of the key elements of Corporate Actions processing * Your knowledge of the Custody industry, Fund Accounting and Mutual Fund Operations * Your ability to manage multiple tasks effectively * Your ability to identify and escalate high risk items * Your strong written and verbal communications skills * Your time management skills * Your analytical and problem\-solving skills * Your Innovative mindset – you look for new ways to improve current processes and develop creative solutions. The **Value** You Deliver * Responsibility for high quality processing of Mandatory and Voluntary corporate actions * Managing the communication of complex corporate actions to internal stakeholders * Interacting with many groups within the Fidelity organization and effectively representing the business to customers and business partners * Escalating issues appropriately * Demonstrating a strong team\-work ethic How Your Work **Impacts** the Organization The Global Middle Office acts as a servicing centre for FMR Co.'s trading desks globally, facilitating the confirmation and settlement of security and currency transactions with brokers and custodian banks, and providing daily cash estimates to Trading. It is also responsible for coordinating corporate action executions, ensuring the collection of income to the Funds, resolving position differences and facilitating securities lending activities. **Company Overview** At Fidelity, we are focused on making our financial expertise broadly accessible and effective in helping people live the lives they want. We are a privately held company that places a high degree of value in creating and nurturing a work environment that attracts the best talent and reflects our commitment to our associates. Fidelity established an Ireland presence in 1996 and we currently employ 1000 staff across IT and shared services, with offices in Dublin (City West) and Galway (Parkmore East). For information about working at Fidelity, visit www.fidelityinvestments.ie. Fidelity Investments is an equal opportunity employer. **Category** Investment Operations

title: Junior Buyer
description: We are currently seeking an experienced and detail\-oriented Buyer / Inventory Planner to join our team in Dublin. This is an excellent opportunity for a motivated professional with strong purchasing and inventory planning experience to play a key role in supporting customer service excellence and stock management performance. **About the Role** Reporting to the Purchasing Manager, you will manage the purchasing process centrally for a portfolio of products and suppliers, ensuring optimal stock availability to meet customer demand while maintaining cost efficiency. The successful candidate will have strong organisational skills, excellent communication abilities, and experience working with ERP systems in a fast\-paced environment. **Key Responsibilities** * Manage inventory replenishment and maintain ERP data for MRP forecasting and stock planning * Ensure replenishment parameters support excellent customer service while optimising stock levels and costs * Build and maintain strong working relationships with suppliers and internal stakeholders * Negotiate and coordinate branch stock cleanses and aged stock reduction initiatives * Monitor supplier performance and gather market intelligence to support negotiations and supplier development * Resolve supplier invoice and debit note queries efficiently * Manage urgent purchasing requests and title: Junior Electrical Engineer
description: Junior Electrical Engineer, Dublin, Ireland Apply Now Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions. We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities, and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Key Responsibilities Of The Role** * To support the engineering requirements through managing \& liaising with Key Project Stakeholders (Client design team, Main contractor, Suppliers, Sub\-contractors, etc). * Information reporting will be an essential activity to ensure activity targets/deadlines are met to support the project team. There will be an Engineering Task Log generated and managed by the Engineering Lead * Familiarisation of Design/IFC specifications and drawings to fully understand the scope of the Project works. Overseeing the Co\-ordination process to transform design information into full installation drawings by BIM personnel ensuring spec compliance and as per schedule priority. This will require generation/management of Requests for Information (RFI) * Assisting the Installation Package team to manage the quality needs for the project on site, providing guidance to ensure we are compliant to all relevant standards/codes and specifications associated. This will include all site activities associated with validation and handover to the client. * Assessment of Sub\-contract and Supplier Tender returns for Technical compliance with design intent specifications. * Familiarisation with the Project schedule and ensuring Engineering activities do not impact on same (e.g. Procurement, etc). * Management of the technical submittal, approvals * Management of the material procurement utilising Engineering Materials Report (EMR) by producing material take offs identifying the lead in periods and field need rates * Ensure Works are installed in accordance with Project specifications and to the Project QA plan (for e.g. pre\-agreed benchmarking). * Management of Sub\-contractors and ensuring adequate information flow to ensure works progress in line with the Project Schedule and Weekly Meeting logged. * Evaluate and promote the use of new engineering practices and processes to enhance delivery time of the project. * Preparation and agreeing project completion criteria and close out documents. * Preparing Sub\-contract and Supplier Tender Packages in conjunction with Project QS. * Manage the Construction Work Packs process from development, implementation and handover. This will involve activities onsite to support our construction management team. * Manage BIM priorities i.e., support team to develop modular concepts, Typical fabrication details and Isometrics generation and to release for construction. Validation of materials/assets within the model are also to be verified for construction. Change management is a critical process to be understood and controlled. * There may be a requirement to attend project meetings or “White Board Activity Planning sessions. This will depend on the needs of the project and your team manager. **Essential Criteria For The Role** * 2\-3 years’ experience working in similar role * Degree Qualification in Electrical Engineering * Excellent organisational and project planning skills * Ability to work as part of a team * Excellent negotiation, problem solving and decision\-making skills * Proficient in use of Navisworks and Database systems related to Engineering functions * Familiar with Project /Primavera planning software and CAD/BIM process * Ability to manage workload and prioritise * Excellent communication skills * Ability to work on own initiative ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer*** **.** Apply Now

title: Junior Process Engineer - Fuze Services
description: At Fuze Health, we put patients first and tirelessly address the most pressing needs in healthcare. We empower millions to digitally connect with care providers, essential health resources and needed treatments – and enable care providers, employers, health plans and life sciences companies to meaningfully enhance quality, outcomes and value. We are dedicated to helping our partners evolve and modernize to meet emerging patient and marketplace needs. Fuze Health’s foundation is built upon the strategic combination of several proven, technology\-powered innovators in the digital health, diagnostics, and pharmacy sectors. Our growing portfolio brings together the capabilities of industry leaders including LetsGetChecked, Truepill, and Alto Pharmacy, to create a distinctive, unified force in healthcare. Together, we have the shared vision, advanced capabilities and talented teams to deliver next\-generation solutions that patients and healthcare partners need today and into the future. **Junior Process Engineer** We are looking for a Jr.Process Engineer for our Manufacturing facility in Northern Cross. You will report to the Senior Process Engineer and become involved in the reactive and planned maintenance and development of facilities equipment, layout and infrastructure. Provide support to the project for Facilities, Process and Validation. We are looking for a dynamic and hands\-on Junior Process Engineer for our state\-of\-the\-art manufacturing facility in Northern Cross, Dublin. Reporting to the Senior Process Engineer, this role offers a unique, fast\-paced opportunity to work across both Cleanroom environments and our continuous flowline production for our personal home diagnostic kits. As a Junior Process Engineer, you will bridge the gap between process optimization and mechanical upkeep. You will be responsible for driving efficiency through Lean methodologies, keeping technical documentation flawless, and working directly on the floor to support our Maintenance team with equipment troubleshooting and reliability. **Responsibilities Include, But Are Not Limited To, The Following** * Duties will be variable depending on the nature of the production needs and ongoing engineering projects. Core responsibilities include: * Process Optimization \& Lean: Support the implementation of Lean manufacturing methods to eliminate waste, improve cycle times, and optimize continuous flowline efficiency. * Work Instructions \& Documentation: Create, review, and update standard operating procedures (SOPs) and Work Instructions (WIs) to ensure operational excellence and compliance. * Time \& Motion Analysis: Assist in conducting time analyses, capacity studies, and line balancing to maximize production throughput. * Maintenance \& Operations Support: Provide hands\-on support to the Maintenance team for planned (preventative) and reactive maintenance, calibration, and troubleshooting of plant and automated equipment. * Cleanroom \& Flowline Support: Ensure optimal equipment performance, workstation layout, and general infrastructure across both Cleanroom and standard manufacturing areas. * Validation Assistance: Support the Process Engineering and Validation teams in executing equipment test protocols (IQ/OQ/PQ) and compiling technical reports. * Automation \& Robotics: Assist in the basic diagnostics and maintenance of our packaging and automation systems. * Health \& Safety: Maintain compliance with safety standards and participate in H\&S programs related to facility and process upgrades. **What are we looking for?** * Education: 3rd level qualification in Engineering (Manufacturing, Mechanical, Biomedical, Process, or equivalent). * Experience: 1\+ years of experience in a manufacturing environment (internship/placement experience considered), preferably within the medical device (ISO 13485\) or pharmaceutical industries. * Adaptability: Comfort working in a cleanroom environment and a willingness to be hands\-on with mechanical/maintenance tasks when needed. * Skills: Strong analytical mindset with a basic understanding of Lean Manufacturing concepts and time\-analysis tools. * Tech Savvy: Competency with documentation tools (e.g., Google Docs, MS Word) and ability to read technical diagrams/drawings. **Nice\-to\-Haves** * Previous exposure to automated packaging lines or automation systems. * Basic understanding of electrical wiring or circuit diagrams. **Benefits** Alongside an hourly rate of **€15\.86 \- €16\.40** we offer a range of benefits including: * Health insurance and an Employee Assistance Programme * Pension matching contribution * Annual Compensation Reviews * LetsGetChecked has a Flexible Annual Leave policy. * Free monthly LetsGetChecked tests as we are not only focused on the well being of our patients but also the well being of our teams * A referral bonus programme to reward you for helping us hire the best talent * Internal Opportunities and Careers Clinics to help you progress your career within the company. * Bike to work scheme \- and secure bike storage onsite. * Maternity, Paternity, Parental and Wedding leave. At Fuze Health, we are committed to fostering an inclusive environment that celebrates diversity in all its forms. We believe that the diversity of thought, background, and experience strengthens our teams and drives innovation. We are an equal\-opportunity employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, ethnicity, religion, color, place of birth, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, military service status, or disability status. Our goal is to ensure that everyone feels valued and empowered to thrive. Fuze Corporate Inc. and its subsidiaries respects your privacy and is committed to protecting your personal information. Please read our Candidate Privacy Notice which explains how we collect, use, disclose, and protect personal information about job applicants during the recruitment and hiring process.

title: Junior Quantity Surveyor
description: Job Description: At IPS, we are global leaders in developing innovative solutions for the consulting, architecture, engineering, project controls, procurement, construction management, and CQV of technically complex facilities, primarily in the life sciences industry. We are looking for a talented **Junior Quantity Surveyor** to join our growing construction team to be based at our EMEA office located in **Dublin, Ireland**. We are looking to appoint a construction industry professional with expert knowledge of construction costs and contracts. In this role, you will be responsible for managing all aspects of the contractual and financial side of construction projects and ensuring that construction projects are completed within their projected budget. The Quantity Surveyor assists with the valuation of construction works, bidding and project budgeting, and the submission of bills to the client, in addition to estimating services to projects and business development efforts. Job Duties and Responsibilities* Prepares service estimates for projects as required to support the achievement of corporate objectives * Prepares constructed cost estimates for projects as required to support achievement of corporate objectives * Supports the design, implementation, and maintenance of estimating programs and systems for the corporation * Works with Project Executives and Project Managers to coordinate the acquisition of input information for the estimating process for projects * Prepares project estimates using drawings, specifications, scope, basis of design, and other project information as input * Maintains files of project estimates and supporting backup documentation for reference during project execution * Supports the evaluation of historical data, including purchase orders, subcontracts, force reports, and productivity reports, and incorporates relevant data into the company unit cost, material, and labor factors * Monitors subcontract and purchase order awards, compares awarded costs to budgeted costs, determines the bases for variances, and adjusts estimating systems, models, and unit pricing to adjust future estimates * Reviews design options and recommends the best solutions based on cost, engineering quality, or availability of materials * Maintains relationships in the industry to facilitate the acquisition of estimating data and processes * Prepares estimates to support the change management process for projects * Works with Project Executives and Project Managers to monitor project implementation for compliance with the basis of the project estimate as required * Evaluates required estimate contents by reviewing requests for proposal, attending pre\-bid meetings, and communicating with proposal team to support preparation of bids and proposals during bid/proposal preparation phase * Special projects as assigned Qualifications \& Requirements: * Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Construction Management, or a related discipline, or an equivalent technical degree. * Significant experience in Project Delivery, including Estimating and Cost. * 3\-6\+ years relevant industry experience. * Exposure to MEP and Cost Estimating systems is a distinct advantage. Skills* Written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to write clear reports to convey complex information simply to a diverse range of people. * Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to build and develop relationships. * Numerical and data analysis skills. * A creative and innovative approach to problem\-solving. * IT skills and the ability to learn sophisticated design and costing IT packages. * Teamwork skills and the ability to motivate and lead those on\-site. * Project management skills. * Resilience, determination, and the ability to work well under pressure. * Good commercial awareness. * A practical, logical, and methodical approach to work. * Attention to detail. * Detailed knowledge of past and current building and construction technology, processes, materials, business, and legal matters. All interviews are conducted either in person or virtually, with **video required**. About Us: **IPS**, a Berkshire Hathaway company, is a global leader in technical consulting, architecture, engineering, procurement, construction management, commissioning, qualification, and validation services for technically complex facilities. **Integration** is how we think and work. It requires the ability to unite existing and emerging technologies into a process that reliably delivers products and accelerates speed to market. That end\-to\-end mindset allows us to be agile and anticipate challenges as we move projects toward operational readiness. IPS applies design and delivery expertise, client\-side experience, and supplier\-side insight to every project. We infuse our curiosity, our passion, and our commitment to partnership in delivering solutions at any scale. We meet our clients where they are and get them to where they need to go. That’s only possible when we care as much as our clients do. Specialties Process Architecture, Process Design, Engineering, Construction, Commissioning, Qualification, LEAN Project Delivery, Strategic Master Planning, Energy Conservation Management, Project Controls, Program/Project Management, Operations/Logistics Strategy and Planning, EPCMV, and Staffing Services. *All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment at IPS without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, sex, age, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetic predisposition or carrier status, veteran, marital, or citizenship status, or any other status protected by law. IPS is proud to be an affirmative action and equal opportunity employer.* *\#LI\-KS1*

title: Kitchen Porter
description: **Join the Heart of the Kitchen as a Kitchen Porter at Carluccio’s!** **About Us:** At Carluccio’s, we believe that every great dish begins with a clean and organised kitchen. Our passion for authentic Italian cuisine is matched by our commitment to maintaining the highest standards of cleanliness and efficiency. We’re looking for a dedicated Kitchen Porter to join our team and keep our kitchen running smoothly. **The Role:** As a Kitchen Porter at Carluccio’s, you’ll play a crucial role in supporting our chefs and ensuring that the kitchen is always clean, safe, and well\-organised. Here’s what you’ll be doing: * Keep the Kitchen Spotless: Maintain cleanliness in the kitchen by washing dishes, utensils, and equipment, and ensuring work surfaces are clean and sanitary. * Support the Team: Assist chefs and kitchen staff with basic food preparation tasks, and help with organising and storing deliveries. * Manage Waste: Properly dispose of waste and recycling, keeping the kitchen area tidy and free of hazards. * Stock and Organize: Help with the organisation and restocking of ingredients and supplies, making sure the kitchen is always ready for service. * Ensure Safety: Follow all health and safety guidelines to ensure a safe working environment for everyone in the kitchen. What’s In It For You? We’re Not Just Offering a Job; We’re Offering a Career Full Of Excitement And Growth * Feast on 50% Off your total bill for you and 5 friends across all our brands—because great food should be shared! * Unlock Exclusive Discounts on thousands of online and high\-street retailers, plus restaurants, through our BRG Spark App. * Stock Up on Favourites: Enjoy 20% off at Carluccio’s retail gift shop \& deli. * Get Paid When You Need It with Wage Stream, offering access to advanced pay. * Refer Friends and Earn with our bonus scheme. * Skyrocket Your Career: Excellent opportunities for career development across our diverse brand portfolio. * Work Your Way: Enjoy flexible working opportunities that suit your lifestyle. This is your chance to be an essential part of our kitchen team, where every contribution is valued. If you’re ready to start your career in hospitality or take the next step, join us at Carluccio’s as a Kitchen Porter and become part of our family! **Boparan Restaurant Group (BRG)** is a growing hospitality group committed to our goal of striving to become the Best Restaurant Group in everything we do. We’re obsessed with building credibility, going above and beyond, and delivering a memorable guest experience and an amazing working environment. **At BRG, We Live By Our Core Values** ✅ **Honest** – Acting with integrity in everything we do. ✅ **Hardworking** – Giving our best, every day. ✅ **Hungry** – Always striving for growth and excellence. ✅ **Heart** – Caring deeply about our people, our guests, and our communities. Carluccio's on Dawson Street offers a relaxed, authentic Italian dining experience with a menu featuring classic dishes made from fresh, quality ingredients. **Skills** Fast\-Paced Experience

description: Role: Lawyer – Property Reporting to: Property Partner The successful candidate will be joining an innovative and market leading firm with a growing practice who will be involved in all aspects of Commercial Real Estate matters. title: Leiter der Finanzabteilung
description: IPG Photonics Corporation is a world leader in the manufacture of fiber laser sources and laser systems. IPG products are used primarily in materials processing and a wide variety of other applications. Our mission is to make fiber laser technology the tool of choice in industrial production. As an international company, we have been developing and producing sophisticated high\-tech solutions for more than 30 years with over 5,000 employees worldwide. To support our continued growth, we are looking for a qualified candidate to join IPG Photonics in **Ireland** as **Treasury Manager (m/f)** ========================== **Your responsibilities:** -------------------------- * Oversee the European cash pool, including cash balances, payments, and related loan administration * Manage treasury operations within the Treasury Management System (Kyriba), including payment processing and reporting * Monitor global cash balances, intercompany funding activities, and foreign currency exposures * Support banking administration activities including KYC, account maintenance, and implementation of banking services * Coordinate with Finance, Tax, and Accounting teams on intercompany loans, dividends, and treasury\-related activities * Support statutory audit, treasury controls, compliance, and documentation requirements * Serve as a board member of the Irish entity, supporting governance and corporate administration activities * Drive process improvements, treasury automation initiatives, and implementation of new banking technologies **Your profile:** ----------------- * Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Business, or related field * Extensive treasury experience within a multinational environment * Strong knowledge of cash management, cash pooling, investments, and foreign currency risk management * Experience with treasury management systems; Kyriba experience is an advantage * Strong analytical, organizational, and problem\-solving skills * Excellent communication and stakeholder management capabilities * Ability to work independently in a fast\-paced international environment * Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously **Our offerings:** ------------------ * Opportunity to work in a global high\-tech organization * International and collaborative working environment * Competitive compensation and benefits package * Professional development and growth opportunities If you are looking for an interesting and challenging position, surrounded by people who share your technical interests, please apply by sending your CV in English. Please also attach documents proving your school education and indicate the earliest possible starting date. References from previous employers are welcome. **Location:** Ireland **Hours:** full\-time **Start:** immediately bewerbungen@ipgphotonics.com

title: Loss Prevention Officer
description: TJX Europe At TJX Europe, every day brings new opportunities for growth, exploration, and achievement. You’ll be part of our vibrant team that embraces diversity, fosters collaboration, and prioritises your development. Whether you’re working in our Distribution Centers, Corporate Offices, or Retail Stores—TK Maxx \& Homesense, you’ll find abundant opportunities to learn, thrive, and make an impact. Come join our TJX team—a Fortune 100 company and the world’s leading off\-price retailer. **Job Description** **Could you be one of our Loss Prevention Officers?** As the world's leading off\-price retailer of clothing and homeware, we're all about putting our customers first. With thousands of shops worldwide, we aim to provide a positive and memorable experience for all. Working as a **Loss Prevention Officer** , you'll help to deliver our goal of keeping our customers, associates and assets safe. Experience of being in a customer facing role, within a fast\-paced retail environment or security guard role is ideal but not essential. **In This Role** You will be supporting a positive customer experience within our stores by being a visible, friendly, approachable presence at our entrance doors. You will be focused on deterring loss on the salesfloor whilst acting as an ambassador for the loss prevention function. You will work closely with the store team to maintain security of our product and together you will aim to deliver effective conflict resolution when required. You will need to demonstrate your ability to remain calm and professional under pressure. You will also be self\-motivated, resilient and driven to make a difference with a natural ability to communicate. **In return, you'll get an in\-depth programme of training when you start.** **Are we right for you?** We're all about helping you develop yourself. The skills you'll learn working within Loss Prevention will help you be at your best. Plus, you'll be surrounded by the support of our fun and caring team. Also as a TJX Europe associate, you'll enjoy such things as: Competitive Salary Uniform Provided Comprehensive package of benefits to support your mental, physical and financial wellbeing such as access to an employee assistance program, associate discount and service awards Join us, and you’ll be a part of our thriving, growing European business filled with opportunities for growth and recognition. We’re proud of our culture, we work hard, have lots of fun, and give back to many causes we care about. It will be a role you won't just like but love. As proud as we are of our past success, it’s our future that excites us most. We strive to provide opportunities for growth, recognition and a competitive salary and benefits package. Share our determination to think bolder and bigger, and be part of our future. We consider all applicants for employment without regard to age, disability, gender, gender reassignment, marriage and civil partnerships, pregnancy and maternity, race, religion or belief and/or sexual orientation. **Address** Unit 1 Main Street The Courtyard Shopping Centre **Location:** EUR TK Maxx IE Store 371 \- Newbridge

title: Maintenance Planner
description: **Job Description** **Maintenance Planner** **Littleconnell Brewery, Co.Kildare** **Permanent Contract** **Closing Date: 14** **th** **June** **Maintenance Planner \- €56,700 \- €69,300 plus competitive benefits package with pension, shares scheme and bonus.** *This is a day shift role working Monday – Friday.* **Join our Supply Chain and Manufacturing team.** Our Supply Chain and Manufacturing team are problem\-solvers, sourcing, producing, and delivering our much\-loved brands to delight customers around the globe. Over half of Diageo’s 30,000\+ people work in Supply Chain and Manufacturing. It’s an intricate and sophisticated operation that’s the product of logistical, manufacturing, and technical collaborations. We are proud to be a global leader in beverage alcohol with an exceptional portfolio of brands, including Guinness, Johnnie Walker, Baileys, Smirnoff, Tanqueray, and many more. A forward\-thinking €200 million investment has brought an innovative, carbon\-neutral brewery to life in Newbridge, Co. Kildare. This best\-in\-class facility is Ireland’s second\-largest brewing operation and a powerhouse of sustainability, running entirely on renewable energy. It’s where iconic beers like Rockshore, Harp, Smithwick’s, and Kilkenny are crafted for a new generation. **About The Role** We are seeking a Maintenance Planner to ensure high standards of maintenance and asset care across our brewery, utilities, and water treatment facilities. Working in a semi\-autonomous environment, you’ll play a key role in end\-to\-end maintenance planning and execution, managing SAP processes and driving reliability, performance, and continuous improvement across site operations. **Key Responsibilities** * Plan and schedule maintenance activities, coordinating labour, materials, and resources effectively * Manage SAP processes, including maintenance plans, materials setup, and system updates (BOMs, equipment, functional locations) * Monitor and report on KPIs such as equipment reliability and efficiency, driving continuous improvement * Control maintenance costs and optimize spare parts inventory levels * Coordinate maintenance shutdowns and lead integration between operations and maintenance teams * Support root cause analysis and contribute to performance improvements and risk reduction * Ensure compliance with health, safety, and hygiene standards, including contractor supervision **About You** You’ll be a proactive and organised Maintenance Planner with a strong technical background and a passion for driving performance and reliability. You’ll be comfortable working both independently and as part of a team, with the ability to prioritise effectively and solve problems in a fast\-paced environment. **What You’ll Bring** * Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage and prioritise multiple tasks * Good understanding of preventative and reliability\-centred maintenance techniques * Knowledge of asset performance, maintenance strategy, and root cause problem solving * Experience using SAP\-PM for planning, scheduling, and maintenance execution * Basic financial awareness, including maintenance costs and budgeting principles * FMCG experience is advantageous but not essential **Working with us** Our purpose is to celebrate life, every day, everywhere. And creating an inclusive culture, where everyone feels valued and that they can belong, is a crucial part of this. We embrace diversity in the broadest possible sense. Feel inspired? title: Maintenance Technician
description: **Background:** If you have experience of operating in a fast moving, fresh produce, or manufacturing environment at and you have a proven track record of achieving results, we can offer challenge and reward in equal measure. **Job Title** Maintenance Technician – donnelly fresh. Foods **Company Background** We’re grounded in a proud history of bringing people across Ireland the best in fresh. What began in Dublin’s Smithfield Market has developed into a thriving business. Providing wholesome, fresh, natural food to families across Ireland is our world. The donnelly fresh group, comprises donnelly fresh. produce, donnelly fresh. foods \& donnelly fresh. farms. The ideal candidate will be organised, self – motivated, ambitious, a good communicator with problem solving abilities. The candidate will also be flexible and adaptable to meet the company’s needs. A team player who is also able to work on their own initiative, with good time management skills. An awareness of cultural diversity is also required. **Job Title:** Maintenance Technician (Electrical \& Mechanical) **Location:** Snugborough Road, Blanchardstown, Dublin 15 **Reporting to:** Maintenance Manager **Job Description:** Working as part of a four person maintenance team, the Maintenance Technician will be responsible for ensuring that the machinery, equipment, services and facility are well maintained and functioning effectively and efficiently, that maintenance downtime is minimised, that production is fully supported and food safety and health \& safety standards are met on a daily basis to meet overall business requirements. **Duties Involved:** * Responsible for implementing the company preventative maintenance programme through scheduled PM’s based on risk assessment and performance reliability. * Respond immediately to breakdowns during the shift, ensuring minimum downtime is experienced. * Carry out detailed root cause analysis on breakdowns to implement corrective repairs and future preventative action. * Inspect, operate, and test machinery to diagnose malfunctions. * Complete all necessary records in compliance with food safety and audit requirements. * Maintain adequate spare parts based on risk assessment. * Responsibility as part of the team for maintaining budget spend targets and pro\- actively working towards cost saving initiatives for the business. * Diligence with Health and Safety and the recording of all relevant documentation * Work closely with the production team supporting them and providing advice, also participating in staff training as required. * Plan all maintenance and preventative maintenance activities. * Ensure full compliance of external contractors when on site. * Be an effective and pro\-active contributor to the team. * Work effectively with all departments, QA, Production, Operations and NPD * Support and participate in business projects for the benefit of the development of the company. **The Candidate:** The ideal candidate will be organised, self – motivated, ambitious, a good communicator with problem solving abilities. Also, flexible, and adaptable as to meet the needs of the business into the future. A team player who is also able to work on own initiative, with good time management skills. **Skills / Competencies/ Desired Experience:** · Diploma or other trade qualification in Electronics/ Electrics · Ability to read and interpret electrical schematics / drawings. · Ability to work on 3 phase, single phase, and low voltage DC. · Wire up various electrical components e.g. MCB, ECLB, RCBO, RCD , contactors \& safety circuits s etc. · Experience of working with process controlling hardware including PLC’s MMI’s · Knowledge of pneumatics is desirable, also familiarity with sensing devices. · Familiarity with motors, reduction gearboxes and fluid pumps · Mechanical experience a distinct advantage · Expert knowledge of a food production environment, preferably chilled. · Good computer skills · English language \- good standard both spoken and written. · Proficiency in other languages would be an advantage. · Good communication skills verbal and written. · A minimum of three years’ experience in a similar role within the food industry · Good attention to detail and problem\-solving skills are important. **Terms of Employment:** · This is a full\-time position. · Five days over seven \- alternate shifts (8\.00 am \- 4\.30 pm) (11\.00 am – 7\.30pm) · On call on a rota basis · Salary – commensurate with experience **Examples of the Machinery/ Equipment:** Ishida multihead weighers/ Sandiacre bagging machines. Ishida Tray sealers Depositers / Blenders Slicer / dicer Eillert vegetable \& salad processing line Vacuum packers Pumps Chilled water system Cookers \- Nilma Printers / Weighers /Metal detectors Refrigeration plant Compressors Generator Job Type: Full\-time Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company events * Employee assistance program * Language training provided * On\-site parking * Sick pay Work Location: In person

title: Material Handler
description: **The Opportunity** **Material Handler** **Location:** Blanchardstown We are currently seeking a **Material Handler** to join a business‑leading company operating in a **GMP‑regulated environment** , including the handling of chemicals and lab related products. This role is ideally suited to individuals who have experience working in **structured, safety‑critical, or regulated environments** and who are comfortable following procedures, quality standards, and compliance requirements. We are open to applicants from a wide range of industries, and **full training will be provided** . **The Team** You will be part of a **small, dedicated team,** working closely together in a highly supportive environment with a positive and collaborative group atmosphere. **What We’re Looking For** **Education** * High school completion or equivalent required * Bachelor’s degree preferred but not essential **Experience** * A consistent and reliable work history is highly desirable * Experience may come from manufacturing, logistics, laboratory support, healthcare support, facilities, production, or other regulated and process‑driven environments * Previous GMP, pharmaceutical, laboratory, or chemical handling experience is an advantage * Candidates with experience working in structured, safety‑critical, or regulated environments will be highly regarded **Additional Skills \& Attributes** * Friendly, reliable, and hard‑working with a positive attitude * Strong attention to detail and ability to follow written procedures * Familiarity with general warehousing or inventory practices is beneficial * Good IT skills preferred (Microsoft Word, Excel, Outlook) **Who You Are** * You enjoy hands‑on work and thrive in a structured, regulated setting * You are comfortable working to procedures, safety standards, and quality requirements * You take ownership of your work and contribute positively to a team environment * You hold a full driving licence and your own vehicle and can commute easily to Meath / Blanchardstown **How You Will Thrive And Create An Impact** * Place stock orders as required, including ad‑hoc and urgent requests * Carry out cleaning and regular decontamination of specialist laboratory equipment * Handle chemicals in accordance with safety, GMP, and site regulatory requirements * Operate equipment in line with procedural documentation and compliance standards * Maintain and update records for equipment servicing, routine activities, and other associated administrative tasks **Why Join Us** * Entry‑level friendly role with comprehensive training provided * Exposure to GMP‑regulated environments and chemical handling * Supportive team culture with close day‑to‑day collaboration * Strong opportunity for career development and progression within a leading organisation **What We Offer** * 37,5 hours per week, only one shift, no weekend work * Private health care * Bonus system * 25 days holidays * Pension scheme * Employee Assistance Program Interested? Let\`s apply! **Disclaimer** The above statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by employees assigned to this classification. They are not intended to be construed as an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties and skills required of employees assigned to this position. Avantor is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. **Why Avantor?** **Dare to go further in your career.** Join our global team of 14,000\+ associates whose passion for discovery and determination to overcome challenges relentlessly advances life\-changing science. **The work we do changes people's lives for the better.** It brings new patient treatments and therapies to market, giving a cancer survivor the chance to walk his daughter down the aisle. It enables medical devices that help a little boy hear his mom's voice for the first time. Outcomes such as these create unlimited opportunities for you to contribute your talents, learn new skills and grow your career at Avantor. title: Microsoft Dynamics Consultant
description: My Dublin based client is recruiting for a Microsoft Dynamics 365 Developer to join the team on a perm basis working on a hybrid model. The Microsoft Dynamics 365 Developer will work on a large scale transformation programme and you will support delivery across several strategics initiatives. * Deliver and support Dynamics 365 Customer Engagement solutions within customer service\-focused environments. * Configure and enhance Dynamics 365 applications in line with operational and business requirements. * Build and optimise automated workflows using Power Automate to improve efficiency and reduce manual processes. * Support structured release management processes using CI/CD pipelines across multiple environments. * Maintain source control standards and collaborative development practices using Git\-based repositories. * Contribute to technical documentation, environment governance, and integration activities. * Configure and customise core CRM elements including entities/tables, forms, dashboards, views, workflows, business process flows, and security models. * Develop custom extensions using plugins, JavaScript, and related technologies where required. * Assist with the design and support of Power Platform solutions, including Canvas Apps. * Keep current with emerging Microsoft technologies, platform updates, and best practices. **Delivery \& Project Responsibilities** * Contribute to project delivery activities in line with agreed timelines, priorities, and objectives. * Participate in Agile ceremonies and work effectively within sprint\-based delivery models. * Manage assigned tasks proactively and communicate progress, risks, and dependencies to stakeholders. * Collaborate closely with technical teams, business users, and project stakeholders to support successful solution delivery. **Skills \& Experience** * Minimum of 5 years’ experience delivering Microsoft Dynamics 365 CE/CRM solutions. * Strong understanding of Dynamics 365 Customer Service and the wider Microsoft Power Platform ecosystem. * Experience designing scalable, maintainable, and reusable technical solutions. * Hands\-on experience developing plugins, workflows, Power Automate solutions, Logic Apps, and Canvas Apps. * Knowledge of Azure integration services and cloud\-based solution design. * Experience working with ALM and DevOps practices, including Git and Azure DevOps. * Strong analytical and troubleshooting skills across cloud and enterprise application environments. * Ability to interpret complex business and technical requirements and translate them into effective solutions. * Strong communication and collaboration skills, with experience working across multidisciplinary teams. * Experience with Azure\-based web applications or enterprise system integrations would be beneficial. * Good understanding of SaaS applications, APIs, and integration approaches.

title: Podcast Producer
description: RTÉ is Ireland’s national public\-service media organisation. We are embarking upon an ambitious and exciting 5\-year transformation strategy to update the way we run the organisation. Our mission is to serve as an independent source of stories, news, information and entertainment for Ireland, reflecting the diversity of its people and providing experiences that enrich the lives of all. This sense of purpose motivates us to deliver to the highest standards in an environment that is collaborative, supportive, and engaging. Attracting and retaining the best talent is key in delivering to our audiences. We want our people to thrive in an environment that values their output. **Meet the Team** RTÉ Podcasts was created to engage with an audience aged under 45 which increasingly consumes media on non\-linear platforms. Our plan is to build habit\-forming podcasts which place RTÉ at the centre of this audience’s digital audio consumption. **About the role:** We are currently seeking to appoint two Podcast Producers to work across Entertainment and Sport. If successful in your application, you will:* Perform a central role in building RTÉ’s podcasting operation, informing its identity as it grows * Work closely with the Head of Podcasting in the process of developing new ideas * Initiate and create compelling, unmissable content for a range of platforms including digital audio, TikTok, Instagram, YouTube and RTÉ.ie * Oversee all aspects of a podcast's production from idea generation onwards \- to include set design, brand design, host and guest management * Manage each episode’s editing, recording, distribution and promotion on audio, video and text platforms, including social media * Be part of a creative team working to strict deadlines \- often in pressurised situations * Interact with people working across RTÉ and the independent sector in the production and promotion of RTÉ podcasts * Work with podcast platforms to increase the visibility and promotion of RTÉ’s podcasts **About you:** The podcasting department is looking to hire people who are innovative, tech\-literate, and editorially brave. Successful candidates for either of the above jobs will have:* An instinctive drive to connect with audiences and an understanding of the younger audience that is currently underserved by RTÉ * A strong sense of what makes a compelling host, guest, topic or format idea * A strong background in digital media production, with an ability to use images, video, audio and text to maximise the impact of RTÉ’s podcasts across various platforms – in particular on social media * An understanding of podcast formats and trends in digital audio * A readiness to learn new technologies as they emerge or become relevant to the work of the podcasting department * An ability to work constructively and positively in a fast\-moving production environment * Sound editorial judgement and the capacity to work closely and creatively with others * An understanding of how AI is shaping the media environment will be an advantage \- including both the challenges it poses and the opportunities it presents. **If you are interested in applying for this opportunity, please apply by submitting:*** your Curriculum Vitae * a cover letter (no more than 300 words), outlining your interest in the role, and suitability to its requirements. * and a paper (no more than 850 words) on one of the following topics:How I would build an Irish version of the Ringer Podcast Network, **or** How I would create an Irish version of the Shameless Podcast, and what other podcasts could be generated as spin offs from that. One appointment will be offered on a full\-time fixed term contract for a duration of two years. The second appointment will be made on a full\-time fixed purpose contract for a duration of 6 months. Both appointments will be made on the Radio Producer salary scale from €54,661 gross pa to €85,602 gross per annum. Closing date for receipt of applications is: **Friday 12th June 2026**. Cuirfear fáilte roimh iarratais ó dhaoine go bhfuil dearcadh dearfach acu i leith na Gaeilge. Applications are welcome from people who have a positive outlook to Irish. Diversity and Inclusion are at the heart of what we do – both on\-screen and behind the scenes. We are committed to building a more inclusive environment and tackling under\-representation while embedding inclusion at every stage of the employee journey. We welcome applications from people with diverse backgrounds, without regard to gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, social experience, race and ethnicity, disability, civil and family status, religious belief and membership of the Traveller Community. We are eager to provide reasonable accommodations for potential and existing employees with any kind of disability. Should you wish to request accommodation for your interview, we encourage you to let us know when you apply.

title: Pre-Sales Solutions Engineer (Italian Speaking)
description: *At Klaviyo, we value the unique backgrounds, experiences and perspectives each Klaviyo (we call ourselves Klaviyos) brings to our workplace each and every day. We believe everyone deserves a fair shot at success and appreciate the experiences each person brings beyond the traditional job requirements. If you’re a close but not exact match with the description, we hope you’ll still consider applying. Want to learn more about life at Klaviyo? Visit klaviyo.com/careers to see how we empower creators to own their own destiny.* **Pre\-Sales Solutions Engineer** *Commercial \| EMEA \| Italian Speaking* **About The Opportunity** Solutions Engineers at Klaviyo are technical sellers at the heart of our revenue motion. We don't just demo software. We work as trusted advisors, helping fast\-growing commercial businesses understand how Klaviyo can solve their real challenges, and then we prove it. We're looking for a Pre\-Sales Solutions Engineer to join our EMEA Commercial team, covering the Entrepreneur and SMB segment. This is a great opportunity for someone early in their pre\-sales career who is curious, eager to learn, and excited by the intersection of technology and sales. You don't need to have all the answers on day one. What matters is that you ask great questions, pick things up quickly, and genuinely want to help customers succeed. You'll work alongside Account Executives across the Italian\-speaking market and wider EMEA region, supporting the technical side of deals and building your skills in a collaborative, high\-energy team environment. **What We Do As Solutions Engineers** We're technical sellers who drive revenue by winning Commercial deals. Our job is to own the technical win. That means building champions, demonstrating clear value, and making sure prospects choose Klaviyo. We work directly with customers to design solutions that integrate into their existing tech stacks, taking a consultative approach that builds confidence and gets buy\-in from both technical and business stakeholders. Our team builds beyond the day\-to\-day too. Recent projects include AI\-powered demo data generators, a Chrome extension for troubleshooting API calls, and templatised mobile apps to showcase our SDK capabilities. We're a team that builds things to solve problems, and we'd love you to grow into that. **How You'll Make a Difference** **Support the Technical Win** * Work alongside Account Executives to support the technical side of Commercial opportunities from qualification through to close. * Participate in technical discovery conversations, learning how to uncover a prospect's goals, current tools, and key constraints. * Deliver product demos and presentations to business and technical audiences, connecting Klaviyo's capabilities to what the customer actually cares about. * Help scope proofs of concept and pilot projects, supporting delivery and keeping stakeholders informed along the way. * Learn how to handle common objections and navigate competitive questions with guidance from the broader team. **Build Customer Confidence** * Develop a strong understanding of Klaviyo's platform, APIs, and integrations so you can answer technical questions clearly and confidently. * Build relationships with prospects and support them on topics like data strategy, integrations, and implementation approach. * Collaborate with Product, Engineering, and Support on RFPs, security reviews, and technical evaluations where needed. * Start building knowledge of the competitive landscape so you can add value in deal conversations over time. **Contribute to the Team** * Help build and improve repeatable assets like demo narratives, templates, and playbooks that make the wider team more effective. * Use AI tools to support your work, from research and demo prep to light automation. * Share what you're hearing from customers back to Product and GTM teams, especially around gaps and trends in the SMB segment. **Required** **Who You Are** * Fluent in Italian and English, written and spoken. * 1\+ years of experience in a pre\-sales, solutions engineering, technical sales, or customer\-facing technical role at a B2B SaaS company. * Curiosity about technology and a genuine interest in how businesses use data and marketing tools to grow. * Comfortable talking to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders and adapting your communication style accordingly. * Good at explaining things clearly, whether that's a technical concept to a marketer or a business problem to a developer. * Willing to run live product demos and get comfortable thinking on your feet when questions come up. * A proactive mindset and willingness to learn quickly in a fast\-moving environment. **Nice to Have** * Some familiarity with web technologies, REST APIs, or data integrations, even if you haven't worked with them professionally. * Exposure to marketing technology, eCommerce platforms (e.g. Shopify, Magento), or customer engagement tools. * Basic experience with JavaScript, Python, or SQL for exploring data or light integration work. * Familiarity with AI\-assisted tools (e.g. Claude, Cursor) and an interest in using them to work smarter. * Any experience in a commercial or SMB\-focused environment, whether in sales, support, or a technical capacity. * Additional spoken European languages **What Sets You Apart** * You ask great questions and genuinely listen to the answers. * You're not afraid to say you don't know something, but you always follow up with the answer. * You pick up new tools and concepts quickly and enjoy figuring things out. * You take ownership of your work and follow through without needing to be chased. * You're excited about the idea of building a career in pre\-sales and see this role as a serious starting point. **How We'll Measure Success** * Contribution to technical win rate in the Commercial segment. * Quality of demos, discovery conversations, and handoffs to Customer Success and implementation teams. * Speed and consistency in picking up product knowledge and ramping into deals. * Contributions to team assets and the feedback you bring back from customer conversations. We use Covey as part of our hiring and / or promotional process. For jobs or candidates in NYC, certain features may qualify it as an AEDT. As part of the evaluation process we provide Covey with job requirements and candidate submitted applications. We began using Covey Scout for Inbound on April 3, 2025\. Please see the independent bias audit report covering our use of Covey here Our salary range reflects the cost of labour in the country where the job post is advertised. The base salary offered for this position is determined by several factors, including the applicant’s job\-related skills, relevant experience, education or training, and work location. In addition to base salary, our total compensation package may include participation in the company’s annual cash bonus plan, variable compensation (OTE) for sales and customer success roles, equity, sign\-on payments, and a comprehensive range of health, welfare, and wellbeing benefits based on eligibility. Your recruiter can provide more details about the specific salary/OTE range for your preferred location during the hiring process. **Base Pay Range In Local Currency** €54\.400—€81\.600 EUR *This role may require up to 10% travel for purposes such as new hire onboarding, client or partner work if applicable, team meetings, and industry events. Travel is coordinated in advance.* **Get to Know Klaviyo** We’re Klaviyo (pronounced clay\-vee\-oh). title: Prep Chef

title: Product Marketing Specialist
description: **Join beqom — where tech meets impact** beqom is a high\-growth B2B SaaS company that provides industry\-leading tools for pay equity and transparency, compensation, and performance management. Trusted by some of the world’s most respected companies, beqom enables HR and business leaders to navigate global compliance and make smarter pay decisions that attract, retain, and motivate top talent. Founded in Switzerland and serving clients worldwide, our powerful, enterprise\-ready products are fuelled by beqom pay intelligence. **Role Overview** As a Product Marketing Specialist at beqom, you’ll be at the heart of our commercial success — shaping how we position, launch, and enable the market around our products. Reporting to the Head of Product Marketing and working closely with Product, Marketing, Sales, and Services teams, you will own the tactical execution of product marketing strategy: from compelling messaging and go\-to\-market planning, to sales enablement and competitive positioning. This is a role for someone who can move seamlessly between strategic thinking and hands\-on delivery — and who brings a genuine curiosity about how AI can make that work sharper and faster. **What you'll be doing** **Positioning \& Messaging** * Work with the Head of Product Marketing to develop and execute product marketing strategies and plans for new and existing products, timed to business priorities. * Create compelling, differentiated product messaging tailored to distinct buyer groups, segments, and industry verticals. * Develop clear campaign briefs and collaborate with demand generation on execution. * Ensure consistency of messaging across all customer touchpoints — website, email, sales decks, and collateral. * Produce high\-quality sales assets including pitch decks, one\-pagers, playbooks, brochures, product videos, objection\-handling guides, and FAQs that support every stage of the sales cycle. * Maintain regular updates of competitive battlecards, objection\-handling guidance, and positioning materials in response to market shifts. * Define and evolve beqom’s USPs in collaboration with Product, ensuring they resonate with buyer needs and market dynamics. * Create monthly analysis and progress reports on product marketing activity, with clear metrics and KPIs. **Sales Enablement** * Design, coordinate, and deliver field enablement programmes that support product launches, messaging updates, and major GTM initiatives. * Partner with Product to roll out product updates in an actionable, field\-friendly format. * Champion a culture of continuous learning and field empowerment across the commercial organisation through sales accreditation programmes. * Develop, maintain, and evolve a central library of enablement content and resources. * Track adoption and impact of enablement programmes and iterate based on feedback and performance data. **Cross\-Functional Collaboration** * Act as a trusted partner to commercial colleagues, ensuring consistent articulation of product value across all touchpoints. * Collaborate with Product Management, Marketing, Sales, and other teams to ensure successful product launches and seamless market integration. * Work in close alignment with the Growth Marketing team to coordinate campaigns, sales plays, and promotional activities that build product awareness and generate leads in targeted markets. * Partner with Sales and Product Management to develop bespoke materials that support growth opportunities with major clients. **Skills \& Experience** **What you'll bring** * Proven experience in product marketing or a closely related role in B2B tech, with a strong understanding of SaaS buyer journeys. * Demonstrable experience crafting messaging and supporting go\-to\-market for portfolio products. * Experience with product launches and go\-to\-market planning. * Experience supporting software product lifecycle communications including general availability and release communications. * Strong customer\-centric mindset — able to identify pain points and translate them into compelling marketing strategies. * Proven stakeholder management skills, including working with senior leadership and cross\-functional teams. * Strategic thinker with strong execution skills — you can move from idea to action with clarity and pace. * An AI\-first mindset, actively exploring how GenAI tools can improve and accelerate your work. * Excellent project management and organisational skills, with strong attention to detail. * Strong written and verbal communication skills. * Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or a related discipline (or equivalent experience). **Bonus points if you have** * Experience in the HR tech space or selling into HR and Finance buyers. * Fluent in French * Familiarity with compensation management, pay equity, or performance management solutions.

title: Project Engineer - Water Networks
description: **The Opportunity** Are you a talented **Project Engineer** with a passion for water network design? We have an exciting opportunity for a highly skilled and motivated individual to join our team at Ryan Hanley, now part of Stantec. You'll play a key role in the planning, design, and delivery of crucial water network projects, helping to shape the future of Ireland's water infrastructure. Your responsibilities will include developing detailed designs for water network infrastructure, including pumping stations and storage facilities. You will also prepare feasibility studies, site assessments, and technical evaluations, as well as create specifications and tender documents for projects. You will coordinate with clients, contractors, and our multidisciplinary teams, and you'll manage and guide others in preparing project drawings and other inputs. Throughout the entire project lifecycle, you will provide expert technical support. **About You** You will have a passion for the water sector and have the following : * A third\-level degree in engineering (Level 8 minimum). * Strong post graduate experience in water network design. * Strong professional and technical knowledge. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. * The ability to work independently and as part of a collaborative team. * A keen eye for detail and strong problem\-solving skills. * The ability to manage deadlines and juggle multiple tasks effectively. * Proficiency in industry\-standard software. **Why Join Us?** * Competitive salary and a comprehensive benefits package. * Access to excellent professional growth and development opportunities. * A collaborative and supportive work environment. * Flexibility to be based in our Dublin, Galway, Cork, or Castlebar office. * Work on impactful projects that are making a real difference across Ireland. If you're ready to take on a challenging and rewarding role, we'd love to hear from you. **Interested? Apply now to join our dynamic team and help us build a better future.** **About Stantec** Whether you’re starting out on your engineering career or are looking for a fresh challenge, Ryan Hanley, now part of Stantec, is a great career move. Based in Dublin, Galway, Castlebar and Cork, we are actively recruiting for team players with experience in any of the following areas – water and wastewater treatment process and network design, integrated network management, structural design, site development, road and bridge engineering, project management and administration. You’ll be working for a dynamic company with an excellent reputation in the industry. Ryan Hanley, now part of Stantec, offers a great working environment amongst friendly professionals with an emphasis on teamwork. It’s a company where your ideas and opinions will be valued and your efforts recognised and rewarded. We have been awarded the silver Investors in Diversity accreditation by the Irish Centre for Diversity for our commitment to quality, diversity and inclusion (EDI) in the workplace.

title: Project Officer (Apprenticeship Statistics), Grade 8 – National Apprenticeship Office -ref no. 588
description: SOLAS, the Further Education and Training Authority of Ireland, was established on 27 October 2013 and its mission is to fund, co\-ordinate and monitor a range of Further Education and Training (FET) provisions that is responsive to the needs of learners and employers. It is an agency of the recently formed Department of Further and Higher Education, Research, Innovation and Science. SOLAS works in close collaboration with Education and Training Boards (ETBs) across Ireland on provision of high\-quality further education and training, equipping learners with relevant skills and knowledge, and providing a pipeline of talent into employment and to the Irish economy and society. At SOLAS, we put the learner, employers and communities, which FET serves, at the heart of everything we do. Working with sister agencies, SOLAS leads actions in key policy and strategy documents including the most recent Further Education and Training (FET) Strategy 2020 – 2024, the evaluation of the Greenskills Strategy , the National Skills Strategy 2025 and the People Strategy 2020\-2024 and others. The National Apprenticeship Office (NAO) wishes to recruit a Project Officer for apprenticeship statistics. They will delivery on actions related to data analysis and sharing, enhancing the quality and availability of data, building the survey capability and output of the NAO, enhancing relationships with key stakeholders, and supporting redevelopment of the ACSS as part of a single integrated apprenticeship system. The person appointed will liaise with the data analysis, digital and IT teams in SOLAS and HEA and with the data and statistics strategies being implemented by both organisations, including the planned publication schemes. A key aspect of this role is to coordinate responses from the NAO to parliamentary questions and representations that are made to us through the Department of Further and Higher Education, Research, Innovation and Science. The person appointed will be the first point of contact in the NAO for these queries. They will be responsible for liaising with other staff members within the NAO to craft and progress responses often working to tight deadlines. They will maintain a file of responses to queries and have responsibility for continuously improving service delivery in this area. This permanent position for which the salary scale ranges from **€47,165** to **€74,437** per annum (inclusive of two long service increments) for a 35\-hour week. Starting pay will be at the minimum point of the scale as per Government Circular E100/8/82\. Exceptional circumstances may apply for candidates with current service in the Civil/Public service. SOLAS offers an excellent benefit package including a contributory pension scheme and the opportunity for further training and development. Applications, using the **SOLAS Application Form**, quoting reference number 588, should be e\-mailed to recruitment@solas.ie. Please complete the application form as specified. CVs will not be accepted. The closing date is 12pm, Monday, 15 June 2026 **588 Role Specification** SOLAS, Castleforbes Road, Dublin 1 D01 A8N0 is the base location for this post. A Blended Working policy is in place, and an arrangement is available to request with this role, a minimum requirement of 2 days per week in the office (Castleforbes House, Dublin 1\). One of the days being the SOLAS Anchor Day \- Wednesday. The use of AI throughout your recruitment process is limited. To understand more, see our AI Usage Guidelines. Please note that as a public sector employee, you must reside in the Irish jurisdiction to avail of blended working, and in accordance with Department Public Expenditure and Reform (DPENDR) requirements, you must be available to attend in person if and when required. **SOLAS is an equal opportunity employer. SOLAS welcomes people with diverse and underrepresented backgrounds to apply. If you need assistance or a reasonable accommodation throughout the recruitment process, please reach out to us on recruitment@solas.ie. Canvassing will disqualify.**

title: Project Planner
description: Winthrop Technologies is Europe's only dedicated data centre delivery partner, offering full turnkey solutions from design through to construction and commissioning. Headquartered in Dublin, we deliver projects across Europe and have become the most trusted name in our sector. Reporting to the **Planning Manager** , the **Project Planner** will be responsible for developing, maintaining, and reviewing project schedules and controls procedures. This role requires strong analytical, technical, and communication skills, with a focus on proactive planning, risk management, and performance tracking to ensure successful project delivery. **Responsibilities:** * Develop, maintain, and review detailed master construction programmes and project controls procedures for live projects * Prepare and present progress reviews and reports to senior management * Liaise with key stakeholders to review project progress, identify risks, and support proactive problem\-solving * Establish and manage project schedule resource requirements across all phases — pre\-construction, construction, and commissioning * Create and maintain progress reporting tools to monitor the achievement of project goals and milestones * Provide performance updates and variance reports, identifying corrective actions where required * Represent the Planning function in client and subcontractor meetings, ensuring clear communication of schedule status and planning issues * Support account management and business development by building and maintaining long\-term client relationships * Take ownership of assigned projects and assist colleagues on other projects as needed * Ensure project planning practices align with company standards and contribute to continuous improvement initiatives **Requirements** * Degree in **Engineering, Construction Management** , or a related discipline * Minimum 3 **\+ years' relevant experience** in the construction industry, preferably within a main contracting environment * Experience working on **large\-scale projects** , ideally within the **data centre or high\-tech industrial sectors** * Strong knowledge of construction methodologies, sequencing, and planning processes * Proficient in **Primavera P6** and/or **Asta Powerproject** ; experience with **BIM / digital construction tools and workflows** is highly desirable * Strong analytical and logical thinking skills, with a sound understanding of programming techniques and typical production rates * Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to convey complex planning information clearly * Capable of working to defined, and often challenging, timescales * Strong commercial awareness and understanding of contractual and project management principles **Benefits** * Opportunity to work with a Market Leader in the Data centre Industry * Supportive career development * Competitive salary package * Collaborative team environment * Pipeline of project growth ***Winthrop Technologies Ltd is an equal opportunities employer***

title: Python Software Architect
description: Stelfox is supporting a SaaS provider operating in real\-time risk management solutions as they look to expand their European presence from Dublin. The CTO is looking to hire a Principal / Staff / Lead Software engineer who loves solving problems by designing systems from PoC right through to writing \& deploying code before handing over to the wider engineering team.
